evals.prompt: review my changes before I push
evals.expected_output: Determines scope from unstaged changes, launches 3 parallel code-reviewer agents (simplicity, bugs, conventions), consolidates findings into a single prioritized report grouped by severity
expectations: Output contains '## Code Review Summary' header
Output lists a 'Reviewers:' line naming the three perspectives (simplicity/DRY, bugs/correctness, conventions/abstractions)
Output identifies at least 2 changed files by name
Output includes at least one 'Flagged by:' attribution line showing multi-reviewer perspective
Output flags the missing 'agents' array in plugin.json

evals.prompt: can you do a code review comparing my branch to main? I've been working on the auth module and want to make sure I haven't introduced any regressions
evals.expected_output: Uses branch diff (git diff main...HEAD), launches 3 parallel code-reviewer agents, produces consolidated report. Falls back to recent commit diff when no branch diverges, and notes the auth module was not found
expectations: Output explicitly states what diff scope was used (e.g. branch diff or fallback commit diff)
Output uses a fallback diff strategy when no branch divergence exists, and explains this to the user
Output notes that the 'auth module' mentioned by the user was not found in the diff
Output contains at least one structured severity section (Critical, Important, or Minor)

evals.prompt: check src/aine-agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md and src/aine-agents/plugin.json for any issues — I refactored the error handling and I'm not confident it's right
evals.expected_output: Scopes review to the two specified files, reads them directly (not via git diff), launches 3 parallel code-reviewer agents, produces consolidated report focused on the specified files
expectations: Output mentions both specified files by name (SKILL.md and plugin.json)
Output reads file contents directly rather than relying solely on git diff
Output flags the missing 'agents' array in plugin.json as a Critical or Important issue
